Hotel Transylvania (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $148,313,048Details
International Box Office $228,827,904
Worldwide Box Office $377,140,952
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $67,953,969
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $32,226,968
Total $100,180,937
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Welcome to the Hotel Transylvania, Dracula's lavish five-stake resort, where monsters and their families can live it up, free to be the monsters they are without humans to bother them. On one special weekend, Dracula has invited some of the world's most famous monsters - Frankenstein and his bride, the Mummy, the Invisible Man, a family of werewolves, and more - to celebrate his daughter Mavis's 118th birthday. For Drac, catering to all of these legendary monsters is no problem - but his world could come crashing down when one ordinary guy stumbles on the hotel and takes a shine to Mavis.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$42,522,194 (28.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.49 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:39.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$85,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,349 opening theaters/3,384 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $222,748,242

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 28th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Aruba)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Australia)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Curacao)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Israel)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Malaysia)
Video Release: January 29th, 2013 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some rude humor, action and scary images.
(Rating bulletin 2236, 8/15/2012)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: Hotel Transylvania
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Letting Go, Monster, Mummy, Romance, Vampire, Werewolf, Zombies
Subgenre: Kids Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Sony Pictures Animation
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Welcome to the Hotel Transylvania, Dracula's lavish five-stake resort, where monsters and their families can live it up, free to be the monsters they are without humans to bother them. On one special weekend, Dracula has invited some of the world's most famous monsters - Frankenstein and his bride, the Mummy, the Invisible Man, a family of werewolves, and more - to celebrate his daughter Mavis's 118th birthday. For Drac, catering to all of these legendary monsters is no problem - but his world could come crashing down when one ordinary guy stumbles on the hotel and takes a shine to Mavis.
